key_points: Food resource improvement involves scientific management of crops and livestock to increase food production
Crop variety improvement includes hybridisation, genetic modification and selection of desirable traits
Desired traits in crops include higher yield, resistance to pests and diseases and tolerance to abiotic stresses
Biotic stresses include pests, insects and pathogens while abiotic stresses include drought, salinity and extreme temperatures
Nutrient management ensures balanced supply of macronutrients and micronutrients to plants
Manure improves soil organic matter while fertilizers supply nutrients rapidly for plant growth
Modern irrigation techniques such as sprinkler and drip irrigation help conserve water
Cropping patterns improve productivity and reduce risk of crop failure
Crop protection management includes preventive and control methods such as biological control and chemical pesticides
Animal husbandry focuses on breed improvement, proper feeding and disease management
Composite fish culture uses multiple fish species to utilize different levels of the pond ecosystem
Bee-keeping improves pollination and increases agricultural productivity
examples: Composite fish culture species: Rohu, Catla and Mrigal
Italian bee species used in apiculture: Apis mellifera
formulas: Hybridisation → Crossing genetically different plants to develop improved crop varieties
Biotic stress → Damage caused by living organisms such as pests and pathogens
Abiotic stress → Damage caused by environmental factors such as drought and salinity
Composite fish culture → Cultivation of multiple fish species in the same pond
Integrated farming → Combining crop production with livestock and fisheries
exam_tips: Focus on objectives of crop variety improvement and desirable crop traits
Understand differences between biotic and abiotic stresses
Remember advantages and limitations of fertilizers
Study modern irrigation techniques and their benefits
Understand composite fish culture and its advantages
Revise roles of animal husbandry and apiculture in food production
